cataloging_subject.label: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or Fourteenth-Century Version
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloging subject is the edition-defined composite represented by University of Michigan item AHA2638.0001.001: Julius Zupitza's parallel presentation of the first Middle English couplet Guy redaction in the Auchinleck and Caius witnesses, the independent Auchinleck stanzaic continuation after Guy's marriage, and the Auchinleck Reinbrun continuation, together with short Anglo-Norman passages inserted where the Auchinleck texts are defective and a three-line Cambridge University Library Ff.2.38 supply in the Caius stream. It is not one homogeneous medieval work, every Guy of Warwick redaction or witness, the whole Auchinleck codex, any other physical manuscript, Turnbull's 1840 edition, Zupitza's separate 1875-76 second-version edition, the three separately issued EETS parts, the 1905 title-quarter-sheet carrier, the U-M encoding and images, or the repository XML.

catalog_summary.title: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or Fourteenth-Century Version
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous authors of the distinct Middle English Guy of Warwick and Reinbrun redactions
catalog_summary.editor: Julius Zupitza, with J. A. H. Murray's Auchinleck collation and earlier text by William B. D. D. Turnbull
catalog_summary.date: Constituent Middle English redactions conventionally circa 1300; EETS parts published 1883, 1887, and 1891; composite title-quarter-sheet issued 1905
catalog_summary.region: England; London is a questioned composition localization and must remain tentative

identifiers.scheme: Library of Congress call number in U-M header

titles.value: The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or Fourteenth-Century Version
titles.supplied_by: Normalized from the 1905 composite title-quarter sheet
notes: This is an edition and carrier title, not an authorial medieval title for one unified work.

titles.value: Syth the tyme that Cryst Ihesu / Thorough hys grace and vertu
God graunt hem heuen blis to mede / Þat herken to mi romaunce rede
Iesu, þat ert of miȝte most / Fader, and sone, and holy gost

date_statements.display: The constituent Middle English Guy of Warwick redactions are conventionally dated circa 1300
notes: This broad work-date statement is distinct from the 1331-40 Auchinleck production date and the fifteenth-century Caius and Ff.2.38 copies.

assertion.notes: D-MER prints London with a question mark; the Auchinleck codex's London production does not independently prove composition there.

language_statements.label: Anglo-Norman French

notes: These are edition-level repair passages, not evidence that the four Middle English streams form one bilingual medieval work.

form_statements.description: Verse romance streams with a prose editorial frame
Short rhyming couplets, with Caius splicing portions of the first and later couplet redactions

scope.description: The Middle English stanzaic portions, excluding the Anglo-Norman Reinbrun repair

form_statements.description: Twelve-line tail-rhyme stanzas in the Middle English portions

summaries.value: Zupitza's edition juxtaposes two Middle English couplet witnesses of Guy's career and then continues with the Auchinleck manuscript's independent 299-stanza account of Guy after marriage and its 127-stanza, imperfect Reinbrun narrative. The Auchinleck couplet stream begins with an Anglo-Norman repair from Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 50, collated against Harley MS 3775; Reinbrun ends with another Parker MS 50 repair. The Caius stream itself splices first- and later-couplet redactions and receives three missing lines from CUL Ff.2.38. These passage-level repairs and shared narrative boundaries do not turn the edition into one medieval textual witness.

The U-M XML retains the 1905 title-quarter sheet and all printed text pages 1-674 but marks the half-title and back matter omitted. It contains three BODY/DIV1 streams: the combined Auchinleck couplet plus stanzaic Guy text, the parallel Caius text, and Reinbrun. The structural split between couplet and stanzaic Guy occurs within the first DIV1 at DIV2 129 and shared printed p. 384 / manuscript f. 146vb.

extent.description: Four medieval textual streams plus an editorial frame. The XML contains 22,672 L in 670 LG, 272 DIV2, 5 DIV1 overall, 663 NOTE1, 1,033 MILESTONE, 6 P, and 694 PB. The Auchinleck Guy DIV1 divides into 7,416 L / 128 LG for the couplet-and-opening-supply section and 3,582 L / 299 LG for the stanzaic continuation; Caius has 10,116 L / 115 LG; Reinbrun and its supplied ending have 1,558 L / 128 LG. These are representation metrics, not normalized critical line counts: DIMEV separately describes 11,095 couplet lines, 299 twelve-line stanzas, 127 twelve-line Reinbrun stanzas, and the 11,976-line later-couplet work partly spliced into Caius.

content_parts.label: 1905 composite title-quarter-sheet and local editorial apparatus
content_parts.summary: Composite title matter, Furnivall's signed explanation for binding the three separately issued parts, section headings, source notices, manuscript milestones, and textual notes retained in the encoding. The half-title and back matter are omitted.

locators.value: title-quarter-sheet and verso note dated 25 Nov. 1905

notes: Editorial prose and title matter are not counted as a fifth medieval work.

content_parts.label: Auchinleck couplet Guy of Warwick, with supplied Anglo-Norman opening
content_parts.summary: The first Guy redaction before marriage as preserved imperfectly in the Auchinleck manuscript, preceded in the edition by an Anglo-Norman opening copied from Parker MS 50 and collated against Harley MS 3775. The English witness runs from f. 108ra through f. 146vb and ends at conventional line 7306 with Guy's dragon victory.

locators.value: BODY/DIV1[1]/DIV2[1-128]; 7,416 L; opening at printed p. 2; English begins at milestone 108a:1; ends p. 384

notes: The 7,416 XML L count includes the bracketed Anglo-Norman repair and must not be substituted for DIMEV's conventional 11,095-line extent of the first couplet version across witnesses.

content_parts.label: Caius Guy of Warwick composite couplet witness
content_parts.summary: The parallel Caius MS 107/176 text on manuscript pp. 1-271. The witness combines the first couplet redaction with two substantial portions of the later couplet redaction; Zupitza also inserts three lines absent from Caius from Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, lines 9234-36, at printed p. 531 / edition lines 9515-17.

locators.value: BODY/DIV1[2]; 10,116 L in 115 LG; printed odd pages 3-629; terminal page break [630]
odd-page Caius stream pp. 385-629; three-line supply p. 531, lines 9515-17
lines 9234-36 supply the three edition lines at p. 531

notes: The three-line Ff.2.38 passage is a direct, passage-scoped source; the complete Ff.2.38 later-couplet work is not otherwise flattened into the Caius witness.

content_parts.label: Auchinleck stanzaic Guy of Warwick after marriage
content_parts.summary: An independent 299-stanza tail-rhyme continuation of Guy's story after marriage, beginning on the same f. 146vb and printed p. 384 as the end of the couplet text and ending at f. 167rb / printed p. 630 with 'Amen par charite.'

locators.value: p. 384 after couplet line 7306 through p. 630

notes: The XML's 3,582 L elements do not equal 299 multiplied by twelve because representation and printed lineation differ; DIMEV's structural description remains 299 twelve-line stanzas.

content_parts.label: Reinbrun, son of Guy of Warwick, with supplied Anglo-Norman ending
content_parts.summary: The Auchinleck Reinbrun continuation, 127 Middle English twelve-line stanzas from f. 167rb to its imperfect end on f. 175vb, followed in Zupitza by a bracketed Anglo-Norman continuation copied from Parker MS 50 f. 181ra-rb.

locators.value: BODY/DIV1[3]; printed pp. 631-674; 1,558 L in 128 LG; final LG is the 14-line Anglo-Norman supply
ff. 167rb-175vb; Middle English ends imperfectly 'Inte Ingelonde þai gonne saile'

evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the encoded title, responsibility, publication data, editorial declaration, source statements, inserted passages, lineation, structural counts, page breaks, manuscript milestones, notes, and explicit boundaries.

evidence.citation: Julius Zupitza, ed., The Romance of Guy of Warwick: The First or 14th-Century Version, EETS ES 42, 49, 59 (1883, 1887, 1891), three complete part scans.
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for part issuance, parallel layout, printed boundaries, textual supplies, editorial acknowledgments, and the 1905 title-quarter sheet.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse 2.0-alpha.1, records 4907, 1557, 2907, and 4908.
evidence.summary: Current repertory evidence for four work identities, IMEV numbers, forms, conventional extents, incipits, explicits, witnesses, and the Caius splice.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Guy of Warwick.
evidence.summary: Current scholarly synthesis for five independent redactions, the three narratively continuous Auchinleck works, the Caius splice, circa-1300 date, questioned London localization, form, witnesses, and subject keywords.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Auchinleck Manuscript catalog, contents, texts, and complete IIIF facsimile.
evidence.summary: Official catalog and visual evidence for the codex, date, exact three target ranges, and shared f. 146vb and f. 167rb boundaries.

evidence.citation: Parker Library, Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 50, complete IIIF facsimile.
evidence.summary: Official facsimile evidence for the direct Anglo-Norman opening supply at ff. 103ra-va and Reinbrun ending supply at f. 181ra-rb.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Gonville and Caius College, vol. 1 (1907), pp. 107-08.

evidence.summary: Complete prior edition used as Zupitza's immediate textual intermediary for the Auchinleck Guy and Reinbrun streams.

open_questions.question: What evidence supports or refutes the Database of Middle English Romance's questioned London composition localization for the distinct Guy and Reinbrun redactions?
next_steps: Review the scholarship underlying D-MER's question-marked place field separately for each redaction; do not infer composition from the Auchinleck codex's production place.

open_questions.question: Why does DIMEV record 4907 list Zupitza's 1883-91 edition for the Sloane MS 1044 fragment when the inspected target XML and three part OCR files expose no Sloane 1044 reading or source notice?
next_steps: Check a clean critical bibliography or consult DIMEV editors before asserting that Sloane 1044 contributes directly to aha2638; retain it only as a collateral first-version witness meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Can complete public or mediated digital surrogates be obtained for Caius MS 107/176, CUL MS Ff.2.38, and Harley MS 3775?
next_steps: Ask Gonville and Caius College and Cambridge University Library for whole-object research copies and terms; monitor the exact British Library Harley record while recovery continues.

notes: The edition's title 'first or fourteenth-century version' is retained as historical naming, not treated as proof that its four medieval streams are one version.
Shared folio boundaries are explicit: the couplet text ends and stanzaic Guy begins on f. 146vb; stanzaic Guy ends and Reinbrun begins on f. 167rb.
The CUL Ff.2.38 relation is limited to three copied lines. The Parker relations are limited to the opening and ending repairs. Harley 3775 is limited to collation of the opening repair.
The U-M language header says eng, but direct inspection establishes Middle English as primary, Anglo-Norman in inserted verse, and modern English in editorial matter.
The NLS contents page's stanzaic start typo of f. 145vb is not propagated; the official transcription, visible boundary, Zupitza, and DIMEV support f. 146vb.
